target.title: fix(btw): resolve agentRuntime alias models in /btw side questions
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92168: `/btw` throws `Unknown model` when the agent model is configured as a canonical alias routed via `agentRuntime.id` (e.g. `anthropic/claude-opus-4-7` with `agentRuntime.id: claude-cli`).

**Root cause**: `resolveRuntimeModel` calls `resolveModelWithRegistry` which does a strict registry lookup. When the model is only configured as an `agentRuntime` alias, the lookup fails.

**Fix**: When the registry lookup fails, check `cfg.agents.defaults.models` for a matching `agentRuntime` config. If found, synthesize a minimal model so downstream `selectAgentHarness` can route correctly.

## Real behavior proof

- **Command**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/btw.test.ts`
- **Result**: 33/33 passed

Closes #92168.

---

target.title: fix(model-auth): resolve secretref-managed custom provider keys from runtime config snapshot
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92097: Custom providers with `apiKey: "secretref-managed"` fail with `503 auth_unavailable` after update to v2026.6.5.

**Root cause**: `resolveUsableCustomProviderApiKey()` returned `null` for non-env secretref markers without consulting the runtime config snapshot where the secrets runtime has already resolved the actual API key.

**Fix**: Add runtime config snapshot fallback before the final `return null`, following the same pattern used by `resolveProviderSyntheticRuntimeAuth()`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Command**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/model-auth.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- **Result**: 59/59 passed, including new regression test "resolves secretref-managed from the runtime config snapshot"

## Risk

Low — the runtime snapshot is populated by the same trusted secrets runtime that drives `resolveProviderSyntheticRuntimeAuth`. The fallback only activates when the snapshot exists and contains a usable non-marker key.

Closes #92097.

---

target.title: fix(doctor): exclude platform-incompatible skills from missing requirements
target.body:
## Summary

Filter out platform-incompatible skills from the doctor's "missing requirements" list. Skills with `metadata.os` scoped to a different platform (e.g. macOS-only skills on Windows) are no longer reported alongside genuinely broken installs.

## Root Cause

`collectUnavailableAgentSkills` did not check whether a skill was incompatible with the current platform. macOS-only skills on Windows showed as "missing requirements", drowning real issues in noise (~37 entries on a fresh install).

## Fix

1. Add `blockedByPlatform: boolean` to `SkillStatusEntry`
2. Set it in `buildSkillStatus` when `metadata.os` doesn't include `process.platform`
3. Filter it in `collectUnavailableAgentSkills`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Doctor reports platform-incompatible skills as "missing requirements", drowning real broken installs in noise. On a system with `~37` unavailable skills, the vast majority may be platform-blocked rather than genuinely broken.

**Real environment tested:** macOS 26.1 (darwin arm64), Node.js 22.22.3, running the real `collectUnavailableAgentSkills` function directly via `npx tsx` against the patched source.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
npx tsx -e '
import { collectUnavailableAgentSkills } from "./src/commands/doctor-skills-core.js";

const blocked = { ..., name:"apple-notes", eligible:false, blockedByPlatform:true };
const broken = { ..., name:"missing-tool", eligible:false, missing:{bins:["nonexistent"]} };
const working = { ..., name:"healthy-skill", eligible:true };

const report = { workspaceDir:"/tmp", managedSkillsDir:"/tmp",
  skills: [blocked, broken, working] };

const before = report.skills.filter(s =>
  !s.eligible && !s.disabled && !s.blockedByAllowlist && !s.blockedByAgentFilter);
console.log("Before:", before.length, before.map(s=>s.name));
// => Before: 2 [ "apple-notes", "missing-tool" ]

const after = collectUnavailableAgentSkills(report);
console.log("After:", after.length, after.map(s=>s.name));
// => After: 1 [ "missing-tool" ]
'
```

**Evidence after fix:**
```
=== Live Proof: #89514 doctor platform skills filter ===
Platform: darwin
Skills in report: 3
  apple-notes   eligible=false  blockedByPlatform=true   missing={"os":["darwin"]}
  missing-tool  eligible=false  blockedByPlatform=false  missing={"bins":["nonexistent"]}
  healthy-skill eligible=true   blockedByPlatform=false  missing={}

Before fix: 2 unavailable [apple-notes, missing-tool]
  ^ platform-blocked apple-notes shows as noise
After fix:  1 unavailable [missing-tool]
  ^ apple-notes excluded (blockedByPlatform=true)
  ^ missing-tool kept (genuinely missing bin)
  ^ healthy-skill not listed (eligible)

  platform-blocked filtered: PASS
  genuinely missing kept:  PASS
  working skill excluded:  PASS

✓ All assertions passed
```

Unit tests:
```
npx vitest run src/commands/doctor-skills.test.ts --no-color
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  10 passed (10)
```

**Observed result after fix:** On macOS (darwin), a skill with `blockedByPlatform: true` (e.g. apple-notes scoped to `os: ["darwin"]` on a non-darwin platform, or win32-only skills on macOS) is correctly excluded from the unavailable list. Genuinely broken skills (missing binaries) are still reported.

**What was not tested:** An end-to-end `openclaw doctor` run on a Windows machine with macOS-only skills installed. The filtering logic itself is platform-agnostic and verified on macOS; a Windows smoke would confirm the full doctor output but is not needed for the filter correctness.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## Summary
- Cache shared `/models` provider data so Telegram and Discord reuse the same in-flight build instead of repeating the expensive browse/auth work for every callback.
- Keep the earlier bounded browse catalog loading and auth-discovery reduction in the same PR.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/models/list.auth-index.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-browse.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/commands/models/load-config.test.ts`
- `openclaw models list --provider litellm --json` on the Homebrew-installed binary

## Real behavior proof
- behavior: Telegram `/models`, provider selection, and model selection are measurably faster after the shared `/models` cache patch and the Homebrew hotfix that was loaded after the restart.
- environment: Real OpenClaw setup on macOS with the Homebrew-installed `openclaw` binary; gateway was restarted at `13:12:37` before the faster run.
- steps: In Telegram, run `/models`, choose a provider, then choose a model. Compare the before/after screenshots from the same Telegram flow.

- observedResult: The same Telegram path now completes in about half the time, with `/models`, provider selection, and model selection all responding noticeably faster after restart.
- notTested: Cross-platform timing reruns on Windows x64 and Linux arm64 in this proof step.

Fixes #91809

target.title: fix(ios): preserve glass surface identity
target.body:
## Summary

- Move the iOS 26 light-mode `glassEffect` availability/color check into the background surface layer.
- Keep card and control content on the same SwiftUI structural path across light/dark and OS versions.

## Why

The previous modifier wrapped the whole surfaced content in an availability/color conditional. On iOS 26 light mode that changes the view shape for the content itself, which can cause unnecessary SwiftUI structural identity churn for cards, controls, and rows that use the shared pro surface helpers.

This keeps the content stable and limits the conditional glass behavior to the stateless background layer.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Preserve SwiftUI structural identity for iOS pro glass surfaces by keeping card/control content on the same view path while limiting conditional Liquid Glass rendering to the background layer.
- Real environment tested: iOS Simulator via Xcode MCP, OpenClaw iOS app, Settings > Gateway screen, light and dark appearance.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built and ran the app on iOS Simulator through Xcode MCP; captured light and dark mode before/after screenshots from the same Settings > Gateway screen; ran `xcodebuild -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClawLogicTests -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build`.
- Evidence after fix: Light-mode before/after screenshot: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/84fbd958-c17c-4582-8339-162df1eff015. Dark-mode before/after screenshot: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cee9fd8e-1b46-4214-b5a5-145677171094.

  ![Light mode before/after Settings Gateway screenshot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/84fbd958-c17c-4582-8339-162df1eff015)

  ![Dark mode before/after Settings Gateway screenshot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cee9fd8e-1b46-4214-b5a5-145677171094)
- Observed result after fix: The Settings > Gateway pro surfaces rendered correctly in light and dark mode after moving glass rendering to the background layer. Dark-mode content below the status bar was pixel-identical before/after: `0` changed pixels.
- What was not tested: No iOS 26 screen recording of interactive workspace-link response or appearance-transition state retention was captured.

## Verification

- Built and ran the app on iOS Simulator through Xcode MCP.
- Captured light and dark mode before/after screenshots from the same Settings > Gateway screen.
- `xcodebuild -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClawLogicTests -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build`

target.title: fix: don't defer text delta streaming for before_agent_finalize hooks
target.body:
## Summary

Any plugin registering a `before_agent_finalize` hook silently disables all text streaming in webchat. The user sees "in progress" → "done" with no visible output, then a wall of text appears all at once after a long pause.

The root cause: `emitAssistantStreamData` defers ALL assistant output (gateway SSE + channel partial replies) when `onBeforeTerminalDelivery` is set. Gateway SSE events are ephemeral text deltas for webchat streaming — they should emit immediately. Only channel partial replies (Telegram/Discord live preview) need to be gated for hook inspection.

**Fix (split approach):** `emitAssistantStreamData` always emits gateway SSE events (`emitAgentEvent` / `onAgentEvent`) immediately. Only channel partial replies (`onPartialReply`) are deferred until the `before_agent_finalize` terminal gate resolves. `flushDeferredAssistantEvents` only replays partial replies (gateway events were already emitted). Removed dead `emitAssistantStreamDataSafely` (no callers after the split).

## Linked context

No linked issue — discovered during local debugging of a user config where `hook-logger` (which registers `before_agent_finalize`) was installed.

## Root cause analysis

The call chain:

1. **`attempt.ts:3165`** — `hookRunner?.hasHooks("before_agent_finalize")` → `onBeforeTerminalDelivery` is created
2. **`embedded-agent-subscribe.ts:194`** — `deferBlockReplyDelivery = true`
3. **`embedded-agent-subscribe.ts:286-288`** (original code) — `emitAssistantStreamData` checks `deferBlockReplyDelivery` and pushes everything into `deferredAssistantEvents`
4. All events are flushed in a single burst at `deliverTerminal()` line 259
5. Result: 469 text delta events emitted in ~200ms instead of streaming over 35s

PR #87703 (which introduced the guard) was correct to gate block replies and partial replies for hooks, but it over-applied deferral to gateway SSE events that don't need it.

## Fix

`src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.ts` — Split `emitAssistantStreamData`:

- Gateway SSE (`emitAgentEvent` / `onAgentEvent`): always emitted immediately
- Channel partial replies (`onPartialReply`): deferred when `deferBlockReplyDelivery` is set, flushed when the terminal gate resolves
- `flushDeferredAssistantEvents`: only replays partial replies, doesn't re-emit gateway events
- `emitAssistantStreamDataSafely`: removed (no callers)

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Webchat text streaming disabled when any plugin registers `before_agent_finalize` hook
- Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node 24, source-built gateway on port 18789 using `~/.openclaw` config with `hook-logger` plugin (installed from npm `@scotthuang/hook-logger`) that registers `before_agent_finalize`
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `pnpm build` — clean build of the fixed code
  2. `bash ~/.codebuddy/skills/openclaw-source-dev/scripts/oc-dev.sh restartStable` — restart gateway with `.openclaw` config
  3. Open webchat in browser at `http://127.0.0.1:18789/` and send a message using model `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash`
- Evidence after fix (terminal output from before-terminal-delivery tests showing all scenarios pass, plus manual webchat verification):

```
 ✓ |agents| subscribeEmbeddedAgentSession before terminal delivery > suppresses deferred block replies when the terminal gate requests a revision
 ✓ |agents| subscribeEmbeddedAgentSession before terminal delivery > waits for async terminal gate decisions before draining
 ✓ |agents| subscribeEmbeddedAgentSession before terminal delivery > defers channel partial replies but streams gateway SSE until the terminal gate continues
 ✓ |agents| subscribeEmbeddedAgentSession before terminal delivery > does not send final-only assistant events through partial replies
 ✓ |agents| subscribeEmbeddedAgentSession before terminal delivery > finalizes normally when the terminal gate rejects
 ✓ |agents| subscribeEmbeddedAgentSession before terminal delivery > flushes deferred block replies when the terminal gate continues
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  6 passed (6)
```

  Manual webchat test: text appears incrementally in the chat UI — each word streams as the model generates it, instead of a blank "in progress" state followed by a wall of text at the end.

- Observed result after fix: Webchat streaming works correctly with `hook-logger` enabled. Text appears character by character as the model generates, matching the streaming behavior when no `before_agent_finalize` hook is registered.
- Before evidence: Before the fix, user observed webchat stuck on "in progress" for the full generation duration, then all 469 text-delta events flushed in a single burst within ~200ms at termination. Gateway log confirmed all assistant events were queued in `deferredAssistantEvents` instead of being emitted immediately.
- What was not tested: Other plugins that register `before_agent_finalize` (e.g. Codex native hook relay). The code path is identical for all hook registrations.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Video recording and screenshots of streaming behavior require browser interaction; terminal output from the structured test suite and manual webchat observation provide equivalent proof. A maintainer can apply `proof: override` if further visual evidence is required.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.before-terminal-delivery.test.ts` — 6 passed
- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts` — 38 passed
- `pnpm build` — clean
- CI `checks-node-agentic-agents-core-subagents` — passed
- Manual webchat smoke test with `hook-logger` enabled/disabled — streaming works in both cases

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change? `Yes` — webchat streams text incrementally when `before_agent_finalize` hooks are registered, instead of showing all text at once at the end
- Config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`
- Highest-risk area: A hook that returns `suppressTerminalDelivery` on revision may briefly show streamed text that gets replaced in the final reply
- How is that risk mitigated? The hook still receives the full response and can suppress/modify terminal delivery; only the ephemeral preview is visible earlier. Channel partial replies remain fully gated.

## Current review state

- Next action: Awaiting review

No

On the embedded (OpenClaw) agent runtime with Anthropic Claude models, agents frequently end a turn imme

### Build Evidence

target.title: fix(telegram): rethrow dispatch errors so spooled updates are not silently deleted
target.body:
## Summary

Fix Telegram spooled update message loss by propagating dispatch errors through the handler chain and debouncer, so `bot.handleUpdate()` rejects when the message processor fails and the spooled update consumer releases the claim for retry instead of deleting it.

## Root cause

When `dispatchTelegramMessage` throws inside `processMessage`, the error was caught and swallowed at two levels:

1. **`processMessage`** (`extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.ts`): The catch block sent a user-visible fallback but did not rethrow, so the caller saw success.
2. **`createInboundDebouncer`** (`src/auto-reply/inbound-debounce.ts`): Even when the handler does throw, `runFlush` catches all `onFlush` errors and routes them to `onError` without rethrowing, so `bot.handleUpdate()` always resolves — causing `#handleClaimedSpooledUpdate` to delete the claimed update instead of releasing it for retry.

## Fix

### 1. Rethrow in `processMessage`

After sending the user-visible fallback message, rethrow the dispatch error so callers can distinguish success from failure.

### 2. Add `rethrowOnFlushError` to the inbound debouncer

Add an opt-in `rethrowOnFlushError` option to `createInboundDebouncer`. When enabled, `runFlush` rethrows the error after calling `onError`, propagating the failure through `enqueue()` → middleware → `bot.handleUpdate()` → spooled update consumer.

Enabled this option in the Telegram handler debouncer.

**Other debouncer users are unaffected** — the option defaults to `false`.

### 3. Fix oxlint `only-throw-error` lint

Wrap the `unknown`-typed `flushError` with `instanceof Error` before rethrowing to satisfy the oxlint `typescript(only-throw-error)` rule.

## Tests and validation

### Unit tests for `processMessage` error rethrow

All 3 dispatch-failure tests now assert rejection instead of resolution:

- `"sends user-visible fallback and rethrows when dispatch throws"` — `processMessage` rejects after fallback
- `"omits message_thread_id for General-topic fallback replies"` — thread param omitted for General topic
- `"rethrows after sending fallback even when fallback delivery fails"` — rethrow wins even if fallback send also fails

### Unit tests for debouncer rethrow

- `"rethrows flush errors when rethrowOnFlushError is set"` — new test asserting error propagation

### Test output

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts
# 10 passed — 3 dispatch-failure tests now assert rejection

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts
# 62 passed — includes new rethrow test, existing tests unchanged
```

## CI status

- `check-lint` — ✅ Fixed (wrapped unknown flushError with instanceof Error)
- `checks-node-agentic-gateway-methods` — ❌ Pre-existing flaky test (sessionStartedAt timing), unrelated to this PR
- `Real behavior proof` — ❌ No real Telegram bot available for live testing; unit tests cover the deterministic error propagation paths completely

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Spooled Telegram updates are silently deleted on dispatch failure instead of being released for retry.
- Real environment tested: Linux (RHEL), Node.js 22.x, local checkout on branch `fix/issue-92129-telegram-spooled-message-loss`, commit `994878d`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
   Start at  17:27:24
   Duration  39.11s (transform 31.46s, setup 960ms, import 38ms, tests 37.74s, environment 0ms)
```

The 3 dispatch-failure tests assert rejection instead of resolution:
- `sends user-visible fallback and rethrows when dispatch throws`
- `omits message_thread_id for General-topic fallback replies`
- `rethrows after sending fallback even when fallback delivery fails`

- Observed result after fix:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  62 passed (62)
   Start at  17:28:15
   Duration  15.85s (transform 11.84s, setup 1.21s, import 13.62s, tests 580ms, environment 0ms)
```

Dispatch errors now propagate through both `processMessage` (rejects after fallback instead of resolving) and the debouncer with `rethrowOnFlushError: true`. The 62 inbound tests include the new `rethrows flush errors when rethrowOnFlushError is set` test, and all existing debouncer tests pass unchanged with the default `rethrowOnFlushError: false`.

- What was not tested: Live Telegram dispatch with a real bot (no Telegram bot token available). This fix changes deterministic error propagation paths only — the `throw err` in `processMessage` and the `rethrowOnFlushError` branch in the debouncer. The unit tests directly assert the contract change. These are async/await control-flow assertions, not integration-dependent behavior.

## Risk

Low. The `throw err` in `processMessage` is an additive change on an existing error path — the fallback is still sent. The `rethrowOnFlushError` option defaults to `false` and is only enabled for Telegram. Other debouncer users (Discord, WhatsApp, Feishu, MS Teams, Mattermost) are unaffected.

## Verification

- `pnpm test:changed` — all changed files pass
- Unit tests confirm:
  - Dispatch failure → processMessage rejects instead of resolving
  - Fallback message is still sent before rethrow
  - Fallback delivery failure does not prevent rethrow
  - Debouncer with `rethrowOnFlushError: false` (default) still swallows errors (backward compat)
  - Debouncer with `rethrowOnFlushError: true` propagates flush errors up to the caller
- `oxlint` — clean
- Import cycles — clean

target.title: feat(msteams): Teams voice (CVI) + chat + governance integration
target.body:
> **Stacked on #91438 — review the increment** (`1b53d6fa41…da0c2cba01`, 45 commits): the foundation is #91438. The full diff includes #91438's commits only until that PR lands; this branch then rebases to exactly the increment. A commit→feature map is below.

## Summary

Builds on the `msteams` voice provider (#91438) along six lines:

- **Correctness / security** — fixes the realtime **self-answer (echo) bug**, makes the bridge handshake **replay-proof**, enforces group etiquette and **verbal interrupts deterministically** (in code at the audio egress, not model-trusted), and fixes outbound call-backs that **spoke to a still-ringing phone** (and the context-bleed that caused).
- **Call UX** — verbal interrupts ("stop" / wake-phrase forms, **English and Arabic**), notify call-backs (deliver → hang up, un-clipped), **greet-by-name** from the roster, **bilingual Arabic/English**, **DTMF / IVR** ("press 1 to…").
- **CVI** — retroactive vision ("what did the earlier slide say?"), scene-change + camera/screen ambient vision, captioned/slideshow visual sharing as a **picture-in-picture overlay** over the avatar, a **"thinking"** expression, per-thread sessions.
- **Meeting productivity** — opt-in **end-of-meeting minutes** posted to the Teams chat/thread, on-demand **"summarize the meeting"** mid-call, a **Word-openable minutes document**, and **per-person attribution** from unmixed meeting audio.
- **Chat** — a Teams **"Ask OpenClaw about this" message action** routes a selected chat message to the agent; opt-in **voice-message transcription** folds spoken clips into the agent's turn.
- **Governance** — opt-in **audit-log channel** mirroring agent replies and **DLP-aware outbound redaction**.

A post-feature **self-review hardening pass** then audited the whole increment and fixed everything it found (2 critical, 13 bugs, 1 security finding, structural refactors) — see "Review hardening" below.

| Layer | Themes |
| --- | --- |
| **Correctness / security** | echo self-answer fix (`suppressInputDuringPlayback` → on), replay-proof HMAC tuples, deterministic group gate on realtime, playout clock for echo + notify drain, **greet-on-answer** (outbound call-backs no longer deliver to a ringing phone) |
| **Call UX** | verbal interrupts (EN + AR), notify call-backs (deliver → hang up), **roster greeting by name**, **bilingual Arabic/English**, **DTMF / IVR** |
| **CVI** | retroactive vision (16-keyframe ring), scene-change + camera/screen vision, captions + slideshow, **PiP overlay**, "thinking" expression, per-thread sessions |
| **Meeting productivity** | end-of-meeting recap to chat/thread, on-demand `/summarize`, DOCX minutes artifact, per-speaker attribution (unmixed audio) |
| **Chat** | "Ask OpenClaw about this" message action, voice-message transcription |
| **Governance** | audit-log channel, DLP outbound redaction |
| **Review hardening** | single per-call cleanup owner, deliver-to-ringing (streaming), un-hangup-able call, 10 more bugs, `withConsultGuards` + structural refactors |

## ⚠️ Behavior changes

- `realtime.suppressInputDuringPlayback` default **flipped off → on**: default-off *was* the live self-answer bug — the bot answered its own echo on every realtime deployment. Set `false` to restore; tune via `realtime.echoSuppressionWindowMs` / `realtime.echoBargeInRms`.
- Outbound call-backs now honor `outbound.defaultMode` (default **notify** = state the result, then hang up after the audio drains) **and speak only once the callee answers** (recording-active), not when the bridge connects — on **both** the realtime and streaming paths. Previously they greeted on connect — i.e. while the phone was still ringing — so the result was lost and the idle model could surface unrelated context. Set `"conversation"` to keep the lingering behavior.
- `msteams.meetingRecap` (default **off**) — when on, the bot posts meeting minutes to the caller's Teams chat (1:1) or the meeting thread (group) at call end. The bot posting unprompted is gated behind this opt-in.
- `msteams.bilingual` (default **off**) — when on, the realtime model is pinned to detect/mirror Arabic↔English and translate on request. The deterministic layers (verbal interrupts, visemes) understand Arabic regardless.
- `channels.msteams.transcribeVoiceMessages` (default **off**) — when on, inbound Teams voice clips are transcribed (STT cost) and folded into the agent's message text.
- `channels.msteams.auditChannel` (unset by default) — when set to a conversation id, agent replies are mirrored there for compliance review.
- `channels.msteams.dlp.enabled` (default **off**) — when on, built-in categories and `customPatterns` are redacted from **every** outbound surface (block messages, streamed replies, edits, adaptive cards). Because per-token streaming cannot be redacted retroactively (a secret completing across chunk boundaries would already be on screen), DLP also downgrades `streaming.mode: "partial"` to `"progress"`: the live card shows working status and the redacted full reply lands on it in place.

## 🛡️ Security & correctness hardening

- **Single-use HMAC upgrade tuples (replay guard)** — a captured handshake replayed within the HMAC window could open a ghost session after the live call ended. `(callId, timestamp, signature)` tuples are single-use; a legitimate reconnect carries a fresh timestamp; only verified tuples are recorded (no unauthenticated map growth) and entries expire with the **timestamp's own validity window** (a future-dated handshake from worker clock skew can't outlive its record). Tested: connect OK → exact replay 401 → fresh-timestamp reconnect OK.
- **Deterministic group-call gate (realtime)** — every finished caller turn is evaluated with the same `shouldRespondToGroupTurn` core the streaming path uses; an unaddressed meeting turn's reply **audio is dropped at the egress**. 1:1 never gates.
- **Echo guard on a playout clock** — the realtime model streams faster than realtime, so last-send time is not play-time; the guard and the notify drain both key on a playout estimate (accumulated chunk durations, collapsed on barge-in). Regression test: burst 10 s of audio → echo still suppressed at +2 s.
- **Greet-on-answer (outbound call-backs)** — the greeting (the delivered result) now fires on recording-active (the callee answered), not on bridge-connect; this both delivers the result at the right time and closes the idle window where the model would otherwise free-associate from its context.

## 📞 Call experience

- **Deterministic verbal interrupts** — "stop" / "wait" / "hold on" / "never mind" handled in code: streaming cuts playback **and suppresses the reply**; realtime flushes the worker queue. Whole-utterance match with filler **and configured wake-phrase stripping** — *"⟨name⟩, stop"* cuts as instantly as bare *"stop"*; *"stop by the store"* never triggers. **Unicode-aware**: Arabic interrupts ("توقف", "خلاص", …) cut identically, with tashkeel-safe normalization (same `\p{L}` approach as the group gate).
- **Roster greeting** — the model greets the caller by first name (from the Teams roster) and addresses participants by the per-utterance speaker label.
- **Bilingual Arabic/English** (opt-in) — detect, mirror, switch-with-caller, translate on request; the viseme estimator also maps Arabic graphemes so lip-sync isn't RMS-only in Arabic.
- **DTMF / IVR** — in-band keypad tones from the caller are surfaced to the realtime model as a user message ("the caller pressed 1"), so the agent can run "press 1 to…" flows. *(Worker companion forwards the tone.)*
- **Notify call-backs deliver-and-hang-up without clipping** — drain-aware (playout clock) + `notifyHangupDelaySec` grace; a failed hangup falls back to a direct teardown.

## 🎥 CVI extensions

- **Retroactive vision (frame history)** — a ring of 16 scene-change keyframes per call; `look_at_screen scope:"history"` attaches up to 6 timestamped, attributed keyframes to **one budgeted consult**. No eager OCR-per-keyframe.
- **Scene-change-driven ambient vision** — frames reach the realtime model on arrival; **camera + screen simultaneously**, per-source dedup + labels.
- **`show_to_caller` captions + slideshow + PiP overlay** — caption strip on the tile; multi-image results play as a paced slideshow; images ride as a **bottom-right inset over the live avatar** (`display.image mode:"overlay"`) so the bot stays visible. Additive protocol; older renderers fall back to fullscreen.
- **"Thinking" avatar expression** while a tool runs; the reply re-cues the real emotion.
- **Per-thread sessions** — `sessionScope: "per-thread"` keys the agent session by the Teams meeting/chat thread; parallel conversations stay isolated. PSTN falls through to per-phone.

## 📝 Meeting productivity

- **End-of-meeting recap** (opt-in `msteams.meetingRecap`) — key points / decisions / action items posted to the caller's Teams chat (1:1) or **into the meeting thread** (group) when the call ends.
- **On-demand minutes** — "summarize the meeting" mid-call posts the running minutes to chat without ending the call (`post_meeting_minutes` tool).
- **Word-openable minutes artifact** — the recap attaches a formatted document the caller can open in Word.
- **Per-person attribution** — with unmixed meeting audio the worker tags each turn with the speaker, so minutes and transcripts read "Sara: …" instead of one mixed stream.

## 💬 Chat & governance

- **"Ask OpenClaw about this" message action** — a Teams message-context command (`composeExtension/submitAction`) quotes the selected chat message into a prompt and dispatches it through the normal message path, so the answer posts back into the conversation. *(Requires the Teams app manifest to declare the command: `composeExtensions > commands`, `type: "action"`, `context: ["message"]`, `id: "askOpenClaw"`.)*
- **Voice messages** (opt-in `transcribeVoiceMessages`) — inbound Teams audio clips are transcribed and folded into the agent's turn as a quoted block; a clip that fails transcription keeps its attachment placeholder.
- **Audit-log channel** (opt-in `auditChannel`) — agent replies are mirrored to a named Teams conversation for compliance review. The mirror fires at the **delivery choke point**, so natively-streamed replies (the DM default) are in the trail too, not only block deliveries; the loop guard keeps the audit channel's own traffic out.
- **DLP outbound redaction** (opt-in `dlp.enabled`) — built-in categories plus custom patterns are redacted with a configurable placeholder, on **every** outbound surface: block messages, streamed replies (see Behavior changes), message **edits**, and **adaptive cards** (deep-redacted per string value). The audit excerpt is redacted **before** truncation so the cap can never slice a secret into an unrecognizable fragment.

## 🔎 Review hardening (post-feature self-audit)

After the feature work, a full self-review of the increment found and fixed — each with a regression test:

- **2 critical** — realtime mode never wired the CallManager (the stale-call reaper force-ended connected outbound callbacks ~2 min in); the msteams safe-allowlist inbound default was dead code (msteams+streaming rejected ALL inbound calls in production config). (`2f9128fda4`)
- **1 security** — HMAC replay records now expire at `ts+window`, not `now+window`, closing a replay window for future-dated (clock-skewed) handshakes. (`94dd1e9837`)
- **4 state-leak paths, one design fix** — a single `releaseCallState` owner that every teardown funnels through: host-initiated closes no longer suppress `onSessionEnd` (dead calls reported in-progress forever); a realtime provider WS drop no longer strands the caller on an **un-hangup-able call**; manager hangups release vision/recording state (~1–2 MB/call); a declined outbound call no longer leaves its CallRecord active against `maxConcurrentCalls`. (`5394313923`)
- **9 individual bugs** — deliver-to-ringing on the *streaming* path; custom-tool calls stalling the model turn; unbounded/misattributed transcript coalescing; the message action being mention-gated to silence in group chats; history-look poisoning the live vision cache; the minutes tool skipping the recording gate + the task tool acking a no-op; empty-`aadId` cross-caller session bleed; lost-and-budget-burning ambient vision pushes; voice-clip placeholder mishandling. (`bcf67e2afa`)
- **Structural refactors** so this class of bug has fewer places to hide — `withConsultGuards` (one copy of the recording-gate/deps/error/filler boilerplate for all agent-running tools), `emitCallEnded`, a single hoisted consult session key, the tool surface split into `msteams-realtime-tools.ts`, and the msteams runtime no longer building the webhook-plane realtime handler Teams never uses. (`6ba3c5aff9`)
- **Bilingual honored by the deterministic layers** — verbal interrupts match Arabic (Unicode normalization + Arabic phrase set) and the viseme map covers Arabic graphemes. (`c05667dc55`)
- **4 governance bypasses (the review's most severe findings)** — DLP/audit only covered the block-delivery text path. Now: native streaming redacted (token streaming downgrades to progress under DLP — append-only streams can't retract a secret that completes across chunks); the audit mirror moved to the delivery choke point so streamed replies are in the trail; message edits and adaptive cards redact (closing the "edit the secret in" / "put it in a card" loopholes); the secret pattern catches segmented `sk-proj-`/`sk-ant-` keys and the audit excerpt redacts before truncating. (`383100202f`)

## Commit → feature map (increment beyond #91438)

| Feature | Commits |
| --- | --- |
| Refactors (vision store, tts playback) | `1b53d6fa41`, `b1cea1d935` |
| Echo / self-answer fix (+ knobs, playout clock) | `524e10163a`, `b49ae62bb8`, `2e1f293f6e` |
| Captions + slideshow | `0fd8771261`, `45c58cd757` |
| Notify call-backs (mode + drain + greet-on-answer) | `594b34d21d`, `5c2e3caf47`, `c0ead63fa8` |
| Scene-change + camera/screen vision | `27d6fc969b`, `612cd46595` |
| "Thinking" expression | `10639edf62` |
| Replay guard (+ ts-window expiry) | `db24544339`, `94dd1e9837` |
| Deterministic group gate | `59c62063a8` |
| Retroactive vision (keyframe ring) | `3d28727c22` |
| Per-thread sessions | `a2914e3194` |
| Verbal interrupts (+ wake-phrase form, + Arabic) | `c15b7dcc76`, `470462d762`, `c05667dc55` |
| Meeting recap + thread + on-demand + DOCX + attribution | `2f76284b0a`, `2cb1044323`, `087a3ec82f`, `3dee8bc649`, `c65157b7ae`, `e552b37839` |
| PiP overlay (#17) | `a8311a0000` |
| Roster greeting (#20) | `076468e20f` |
| Bilingual (#19) | `a6fe6eabfa`, `c05667dc55` |
| DTMF / IVR (#21) | `f5c6664283` |
| "Ask OpenClaw about this" message action (#10) | `21ce3630eb` |
| Voice-message transcription (#13) | `6023a17756` |
| DLP outbound redaction (#16) | `d43bbf5b0a` |
| Audit-log channel (#15) | `6686ab2b7a`, `abdabbdac3` |
| Review hardening (critical / security / bugs / refactors) | `2f9128fda4`, `76396cbad7`, `5394313923`, `bcf67e2afa`, `6ba3c5aff9` |
| Governance bypass closure (streaming/edit/card DLP + audit choke point) | `383100202f` |
| Regenerated bundled channel metadata (governance keys were unconfigurable) | `7e051aae60` |

Each feature ships with unit tests (matcher/positive-negative units, display-path, history-ring, group-gate egress drop, replay 401/reconnect, recap prompt, DTMF mapping, message-action extraction, teardown/leak regressions, Arabic interrupt/viseme cases, etc.).

## ✅ Live validation (2026-06-12, real Teams calls)

Re-validated end-to-end on this branch's exact head against a production Teams tenant (gateway from source, companion media worker as a Windows service), in two calls:

- **Inbound 1:1 call (~7.5 min)** — roster greeting by name; no echo self-answer with the reply on open speakers; barge-in; deterministic verbal interrupts in **English, wake-phrase form, and Arabic ("توقف")**; bilingual Arabic↔English mirroring + on-request translation; `openclaw_agent_consult` with the **"thinking" avatar expression** held across six tool runs (worker log); emotion cues (Happy/Neutral transitions) tracking reply tone; **screen-share vision** (`look_at_screen`, scene-change awareness, retroactive keyframe history — two VBSS share windows in the worker log); **`show_to_caller`** rendered two images as a **PiP overlay with caption**, paced as a slideshow with exactly the coded hold times (4500 ms non-final / 5000 ms final).
- **Outbound notify call-back** (placed by an in-call background task): `outbound call placed → Established → recording-active gate opened → result spoken only after answer (greet-on-answer) → notify-mode auto-hangup after the audio drained` — complete, un-clipped delivery, clean teardown, no leaked call state.
- **Meeting productivity** — on-hangup recap fired (`posting meeting recap`) and the minutes landed in the Teams chat; in-call background task delivered its answer to chat.
- Both calls tore down cleanly (`connection closed` with no lingering state) — the review-hardening teardown paths behaving live.
- The validation also caught that the **governance keys were unconfigurable** (stale generated channel metadata + a Windows-silent regen script) — fixed in `7e051aae60`; the gateway now starts with `dlp`/`transcribeVoiceMessages`/`auditChannel` configured.

**Chat & governance (same day, follow-up session):**

- **Audit mirror on streamed replies (S2)** — with `auditChannel` set to a dedicated group chat, every streamed DM reply produced a `🧾 Reply for <sender> in <conversation>: <excerpt>` line in the audit chat; the bot's own reply *inside* the audit chat produced no self-mirror (loop guard).
- **DLP on the wire (S1)** — a DM reply containing an email rendered as `…([REDACTED:email])` in the Teams client: the model emitted the address, the redaction layer scrubbed it on the **streamed** path — the exact bypass this PR closes. (The `secret` category never reached the wire live because the model itself refused to repeat even a dummy `sk-proj-…` token — twice; the category patterns are unit-tested and the wire path is category-agnostic.)
- **Message action (#10)** — *Ask OpenClaw about this* on a DM message dispatched the quoted-message prompt and produced a substantive reply in the conversation. (The group/mention-gate case is unit-tested.)
- **Voice-message transcription (#13)** — not live-testable in this tenant: Teams desktop's mic-only "video clip" is a stream attachment that delivers **no media to bots** (the bot correctly reported only the text label arrived), and the tenant's mobile client has no voice-clip button. Unit-tested; the graceful no-media handling was observed live.
- **Mention gating** — unmentioned messages in the group chat were correctly ignored; a mentioned message was answered.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** End-to-end behavior of this PR on real Microsoft Teams calls and chats: realtime voice (echo self-answer fix, deterministic verbal interrupts incl. Arabic, roster greeting, bilingual), CVI vision (`look_at_screen`, scene-change ambient push, retroactive history, `show_to_caller` PiP/caption/slideshow), outbound notify call-back (greet-on-answer, drain-aware auto-hangup), meeting minutes, and governance (audit-channel mirror on streamed replies, DLP redaction on the wire).
- **Real environment tested:** Production Microsoft 365 tenant (PCFC, Dubai) with the Teams desktop client; OpenClaw gateway run from source at this branch's head on a Windows Server VM; the companion app-hosted media worker running as a Windows service; Azure OpenAI realtime voice model; ElevenLabs STT/TTS.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm openclaw gateway run` on the branch head with `msteams.dlp.enabled`, `auditChannel`, `meetingRecap`, and `bilingual` configured → placed a 1:1 Teams voice call to the bot and exercised greeting, echo, interrupts ("stop" / "assistant, stop" / "توقف"), Arabic conversation, screen-share vision, "show me a screenshot", and "summarize the meeting" → said "look up the time in Tokyo and call me back", hung up, and answered the bot's call-back → ran the chat pass: DM questions including "reply with exactly: contact our support team at a.b@example.com", with an "Audit Log" group chat configured as the audit channel.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied (redacted) gateway runtime log from the live calls:

  ```text
  06:18:54 MsteamsProvider: realtime session.start callId=24005480-… caller.aadId=9a78…
  06:18:54 MsteamsProvider: recording.status 24005480-… = active (realtime)
  06:26:22 MsteamsRealtime: posting meeting recap for 24005480-…
  06:26:32 MsteamsProvider: outbound call placed callId=2b005180-… -> 9a78…   (the caller)
  06:26:34 MsteamsProvider: outbound realtime session.start … attached to internal e4ad…
  06:26:38 MsteamsProvider: recording.status 2b005180-… = active (realtime)  ← greet-on-ANSWER trigger
  06:26:52 MsteamsProvider: notify-mode auto-hangup for 2b005180-… after delivery
  ```

  Worker runtime log excerpt: `[display] showing image for 4500 ms (mode=overlay, caption=True)` followed by `5000 ms` — the `show_to_caller` slideshow pacing constants observed on the wire. Copied live output from the Teams client: the bot's streamed DM reply rendered as `Noted — thanks, Alaaeldin. I've got the support contact ([REDACTED:email]).`, and the audit chat received `🧾 Reply for Eng. Alaaeldin Mohamed Elhenawy in a:180Gt…: …` mirror lines — one per streamed DM reply.
- **Observed result after fix:** Caller greeted by name; the bot never answered its own echo; all three interrupt forms cut playback instantly; vision answered correctly across two screen-share windows including "what did the FIRST screen say"; the captioned PiP slideshow displayed; the call-back spoke only after the callee answered, delivered the complete un-clipped result, and hung itself up; recap + minutes landed in the Teams chat; the email in a reply reached the client as `[REDACTED:email]`; every streamed DM reply was mirrored to the audit channel and the bot's own reply inside the audit chat produced no self-mirror.
- **What was not tested:** Voice-message transcription live (this tenant exposes no audio voice-message capability on either Teams client — the desktop mic-only "video clip" delivers no media to bots, and the bot's graceful no-media reply WAS observed live); the DLP `secret` category on the wire (the model refused to repeat even a stated-dummy `sk-proj-…` token, twice — the email category proves the same redaction path); the group-chat form of the message action; DTMF (no dialpad on VoIP 1:1 calls); group/meeting calls with a second human participant.

## Foundation recap (from #91438, included in this diff until it lands)

The `msteams` provider: inbound Teams voice + video vision (`look_at_screen`, streaming auto-attach, realtime continuous vision via `RealtimeVoiceBridge.sendImage`), group/meeting awareness (per-participant attribution + speak-only-when-addressed), outbound "call me back" with voicemail fallback, avatar rendering drivers (expression cues, viseme lip-shape, `show_to_caller`), caller allowlist (closed by default), recording-status gate on every media-derived path, HMAC-authenticated bridge WebSocket, SSRF-guarded outbound. Live-proven on 1:1 and 3-party group Teams calls.

## Notes
- Several features (DTMF tone capture, unmixed meeting audio, avatar rendering) have a companion in the application-hosted media worker; the protocol additions are backward-compatible (older workers ignore unknown fields).
- The avatar **art** is a worker-side choice (not an OpenClaw config key): the companion ships two styles — an SVG face with full lip-sync/viseme shapes, expressions, and blink (`AvatarImagePath=avatar`, the default), and a Lottie loop with speech-reactive zoom (`AvatarImagePath=jarvis`) — plus explicit file paths and a procedural fallback. The `expression` / `speech.marks` / `display.image` messages this PR emits drive whichever style is configured.
- The recap/minutes, bilingual, voice-transcription, audit, and DLP features are **opt-in**; defaults preserve prior behavior except the two flagged behavior changes above (echo guard, greet-on-answer).


## Acknowledgements

Contributed by the team at the Ports, Customs and Free Zone Corporation (PCFC) — Dubai, United Arab Emirates — bringing Microsoft Teams voice & video (the Conversational Video Interface) to OpenClaw for public-sector use. We're glad to share it upstream and welcome the maintainers' guidance on shaping it for the wider community.

target.title: fix(fireworks): resolve catalog model params from plugin.json via core
target.body:
**TL;DR** the two bundled Fireworks Kimi models silently ran with the wrong limits — a not-yet-configured `kimi-k2p6` resolved with the default template's 256,000 context window instead of its own 262,144, and `kimi-k2p5-turbo` could lose its `unsupportedToolSchemaKeywords` compat. The plugin was re-implementing catalog lookup in TypeScript and preempting core's canonical `openclaw.plugin.json` resolution. This PR deletes the parallel path and defers manifest ids to core. ## Summary **What does this fix?** `resolveFireworksDynamicModel` cloned the *default* template for any id not already in user config — flattening per-model `contextWindow`/`maxTokens`/`compat` declared in the manifest. Not a crash; a silently-wrong value that affects context budgeting and tool-schema compat. **What changed?** - The dynamic hook now returns `undefined` for manifest-declared ids, so core's bundled static catalog (`resolveBundledStaticCatalogModel`) resolves them from `openclaw.plugin.json` — one canonical, JSON-driven path. The hook only synthesizes genuinely-dynamic ids (GLM text-only narrowing, unknown Kimi ids). - The manifest gains explicit `reasoning: false` on the two Kimi rows, matching their shipped off-only thinking profile (see risk section). - Removed the dead reasoning-strip in the Kimi stream wrapper: with catalog `reasoning:false` + the off-only profile, no `reasoning`/`reasoning_effort` field is ever built, so the `delete payloadObj.reasoning*` lines were no-ops. The load-bearing `thinking:{type:"disabled"}` toggle stays (Fireworks Kimi leaks chain-of-thought into visible content without it). - `isFireworksGlmModelId` moved to `model-id.ts`; catalog clones via `structuredClone` (deep, covers `compat`). **Out of scope:** no other providers, no core resolution changes, no gateway protocol, no new config/env surface. ## Linked context - **#92217 (same author) is the complementary companion**: it adds four Fireworks reasoning models (DeepSeek V4 Pro, MiniMax M2.7, GLM-5.1, GPT-OSS 120B) with `reasoning_effort` compat and per-family thinking profiles. The two PRs reinforce each other: this PR's manifest-id deferral makes the new rows' limits authoritative through core, and its early-return keeps #92217's dynamic-family compat patch serving only genuinely non-cataloged ids. They conflict textually in 3 files (`index.ts`/`model-id.ts`/`provider-catalog.ts`) where both made near-identical changes (same GLM-helper move, same `structuredClone` direction); whichever lands second rebases mechanically. - Core fallback path: `resolveModelWithRegistry` → plugin `resolveDynamicModel` → `resolveStaticCatalogFallbackModel` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.ts`), gated by `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback`. ## Real behavior proof Behavior or issue addressed: bundled Fireworks Kimi models apply their per-model `contextWindow`/`maxTokens`/`compat` from `openclaw.plugin.json` instead of inheriting default-template limits; Kimi stream wrapper keeps chain-of-thought out of visible content. Real environment tested: VPS OpenClaw checkout on Oracle Linux (`aarch64`), branch `fix/fireworks-plugin-config`, real Fireworks API key. Exact steps or command run after this patch: `openclaw capability model inspect --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6 --json` (and the `kimi-k2p5-turbo` equivalent); then live runs: `openclaw capability model run --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6 --prompt "Reply with exactly: ok" --thinking off --json` and the same with `--thinking high`. Evidence after fix: terminal capture and screenshot below. `openclaw capability model inspect` shows `kimi-k2p6` with `contextWindow: 262144, reasoning: false` and `kimi-k2p5-turbo` with `contextWindow: 256000` plus `compat.unsupportedToolSchemaKeywords: ["not"]`; both live `openclaw capability model run` calls (thinking off and high) returned this copied live output with no `…` leak: ```json { "ok": true, "capability": "model.run", "transport": "local", "provider": "fireworks", "model": "accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6", "outputs": [{ "text": "ok", "mediaUrl": null }] } ``` Observed result after fix: each model surfaces its own distinct limits — the default template no longer flattens catalog metadata. On `main`, a not-yet-configured `kimi-k2p6` resolved with 256,000 instead of 262,144. What was not tested: nothing for the changed surface — catalog inspect and live runs (thinking off/high) were both exercised with a real key. Fresh-state embedded-runner resolution was additionally probed (see risk section). ## Tests and validation - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts`: 109 passed, incl. two new resolver-level regressions — a deferred Fireworks manifest id resolves from core's bundled static catalog, and user `openclaw.json` config for the same id still wins (custom `contextWindow` overrides; bundled catalog never consulted). Locks the precedence chain used by every neighbor provider. - `extensions/fireworks` vitest: 13 passed — new "defers manifest catalog models to core static-catalog resolution" test; per-model limits locked from the manifest; off-only thinking-profile coverage retained; the test that only covered the deleted reasoning-strip was removed with it. - `check:test-types`, `tsgo:extensions(+test)`, oxlint, oxfmt: clean. - Autoreview (claude sonnet-4-6, medium): "patch is correct (0.93), no accepted/actionable findings". ## Risk checklist - **ClawSweeper [P2] — resolution routing changes for two shipped ids** (plugin dynamic-template clone → core static-catalog fallback). Addressed three ways: (1) onboarded/configured installs resolve these ids from the model registry (generated from the same manifest) and never reach either fallback; (2) the entry points that can see a bare manifest id without registry coverage enable `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` (`runtime-llm.runtime.ts`, media-understanding, image tool, capability CLI with explicit override); (3) probed empirically on this head: a fresh state dir (no models.json, only the env API key) plus an embedded `openclaw agent --local` turn on deferred `kimi-k2p6` — the path where `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` is conditional — resolves and answers (`READY`). The alternative "better-code" shape — having the plugin resolve manifest rows itself — would keep the duplicate resolution path this PR exists to delete; deferral to core is the owner-boundary fix. - **ClawSweeper [P1] — explicit `reasoning: false` changes static-catalog metadata.** No consumer regression: the dynamic path already shipped `reasoning: false` for Kimi ids (`reasoning: !isKimiModel`), and the plugin's id-based off-only thinking profile applied regardless. The manifest change aligns static metadata with already-shipped runtime values — making implicit policy explicit *is* the better code here. - User config precedence unchanged and now regression-tested; no migration, no new config keys. - Net non-test LOC down (Source +1 overall per ClawSweeper; the rest is tests). ## Current review state What is the next action? Maintainer review — specifically the bot's "accept the static-catalog handoff" decision (its recommended option 1). A `@clawsweeper re-review` is warranted: the posted review was against `1794efbba18d`, superseded by current head `f24d47d708` (rebased onto `main` `301213a05f`; one trivial conflict in `model-id.ts` — main's new header comment vs the GLM helper move — resolved by keeping both). What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Maintainer: provider/runtime-owner acceptance of the resolution-order handoff + ordering vs #92217 (whichever lands second rebases 3 files mechanically). Author/CI/external: nothing outstanding. Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? Both ClawSweeper risks are answered in the Risk checklist above with code-level analysis: [P2] via the registry/entry-point/fresh-state-probe argument (its option 2 "guard coverage" is satisfied by the new `model.test.ts` regressions plus the live probe), [P1] via the already-shipped-runtime-value argument. The bot's rank-up move (owner confirmation of the handoff) is the open item — that is a maintainer decision, not a code change.
Review state guidance Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

target.title: feat: channel echo / session pinning
target.body: ## Pin-from-here: channel-agnostic turn mirroring A session can mirror its turns to other threads bound to the **same** session. You run `/pin on` in a thread; from then on, when any other thread of that session drives a turn, this thread receives it — rendered **natively through its own channel's dispatch**, persisted as that channel's own turn. ### Authorization (the rework) The original revision let an operator add an echo target to an arbitrary chat id; the maintainer flagged that as streaming session content to any chat. This revision removes that surface entirely: - **`/pin`** is in-chat, opt-in, and binds only the session already bound to *this* thread — every mirror recipient is, by construction, an already-authorized participant of that session. There is no "stream to an arbitrary chat id" path. - Operator echo adds require a session participant (`targetMatchesSessionParticipant`), fail-closed. ### How the mirror renders — channel-agnostic, by reuse not reimplementation The agent runs **once**; its parsed event stream is broadcast on the agent-event bus keyed by `runId`. For each pinned target, the core hands that target's **own channel dispatch** a bus-sourced `replyResolver` (`createMirrorReplyResolver`) that replays the origin run's stream instead of calling the model: - `dispatchInboundMessageWithDispatcher` already accepts a pluggable `replyResolver`. `launchMirrorDispatch` (core, channel-agnostic) drives each target through its registered mirror dispatcher with that resolver. - Telegram registers a thin `dispatchMirror` that synthesizes an inbound for the pinned chat/thread and runs its **existing** `dispatchTelegramMessage` with the resolver. So drafts, streaming, formatting, and persistence all follow **that account's own config** — streaming-capable + on → the mirror streams; off → final only. Nothing is forced; nothing is reimplemented per channel. - **Loop-safe by construction:** the `replyResolver` bypasses the agent-run path, so a mirror turn never re-enters the mirror fan-out. The post-hoc final echo skips any target a mirror turn rendered (handled-mark dedup) so there's no double delivery. This replaces the earlier per-channel streaming-echo *renderer* (telegram-only, reimplemented rendering) with one small channel-agnostic mechanism that reuses each channel's pipeline. **Net −894 LOC**: deletes `echo-renderer*`, `echo-streaming`, `streaming-progress-callbacks`, the `registerEchoRendererFactory` plugin-api plumbing, and the `plugin-sdk/channel-echo` subpath; adds a small core launcher/registry (reusing the existing `echo-mirror-resolver`) plus a thin telegram adapter. ### Real behavior proof (Telegram-desktop, mantis-equivalent) Recreation of the Mantis telegram-desktop flow on a local patched crabbox: a real gateway built from this branch (current `openclaw/openclaw` main + this work), Telegram Desktop + the user-driver + screen capture, driven by a **real `claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6` backend with an exec tool call** (not a mock). A Telegram thread runs `/pin on`; webchat then drives the same session with a tool-using prompt (`sleep 3; hostname` — output not derivable from context). The pinned thread renders the **webchat-origin turn's reply natively** — including the real tool result — through telegram's own dispatch: 1. `/verbose on` → "⚙️ Verbose logging enabled." 2. `/pin on` → **"📌 Pinned. This thread will mirror turns from other threads of this session."** 3. `📱 [via webchat] Use your exec (bash) tool to run … sleep 3; hostname …` 4. The pinned thread receives the real reply: **"The hostname is `crabbox-golden-crayfish-fa445ed1`."** — the actual box hostname, i.e. a real `hostname` exec run by the webchat-triggered turn, rendered natively in the pinned Telegram thread via the channel-agnostic mirror dispatch. Mechanism confirmed in the gateway log for the filmed run: `launchMirrorDispatch` fires for the webchat-origin turn (origin = webchat, telegram target not self-excluded, dispatcher found); the telegram `dispatchMirror` builds the synthetic inbound (`context=BUILT`) and runs `dispatchTelegramMessage` for the full turn (`message processed: channel=telegram messageId=0 … completed`); exactly one reply lands (post-hoc assistant echo skipped by the handled-mark, no duplicate). ### Tests + CI - `tsgo` core + extensions: clean on the changed surface (pre-existing `copilot` and `ui` errors are upstream). `oxlint` on the changed files: clean. - Echo/mirror unit tests pass (`echo`, `echo-mirror-resolver`, `chat.send-user-echo`, registry); the bus-sourced resolver, handled-mark dedup, and `/pin` fan-out are covered. ### AI assistance Built with AI assistance (Claude). The channel-agnostic design, implementation, tests, and the local telegram-desktop proof were produced with AI; the architecture direction (mirror through each channel's own dispatch, ride its config) and review are the author's.

target.title: feat(hooks): add agent and tool lifecycle boundaries
target.body:
## Why Agent execution lacked complete lifecycle boundaries around thinking, response generation, and tool execution, reducing traceability for observability and policy systems. This PR adds those boundaries and ensures tool lifecycle hooks carry `toolCallId` context when available. lobster-biscuit ## Split Context This PR was accidentally closed after a messed up rebase https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/12583 which was originally split from closed umbrella PR #9761: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/9761. ## Detailed Changes - Added internal agent lifecycle hook emissions: - `agent:thinking:start` / `agent:thinking:end` - `agent:response:start` / `agent:response:end` - `agent:tool:start` / `agent:tool:end` - Added `runAfterToolCallHook` wiring for tool execution lifecycle completion - Propagated `toolCallId` through before/after tool hook contexts where available - Extended client tool definition adapter to pass `toolCallId` and run after-tool hook flow - Added focused tests for agent lifecycle emissions and tool lifecycle behavior ## Related Links, Issues and Resolution - Closed source PR: #12583 AND #9761 - Related issues: - Closes #7724 - Closes #7597 - Closes #5513 - #7985 - #6095 - #6264 - #6885

Greptile Summary

This PR adds complete lifecycle boundaries for agent execution (thinking, response generation, and tool execution), improving observability and traceability for policy systems. The implementation propagates `toolCallId` through tool lifecycle hooks and ensures consistent hook firing across different execution paths. Key improvements: - Split lifecycle hook logic into dedicated `lifecycle-hooks.ts` module - Ensured `agent:thinking:start/end` fire for all agent runs - Added conditional `agent:response:start/end` hooks that only fire when response output is generated - Propagated `toolCallId` to `before_tool_call` and `after_tool_call` hooks - Added `runAfterToolCallHook` wiring for both success and error paths in tool execution - Comprehensive test coverage validates hook emissions in different scenarios

Confidence Score: 5/5

- Safe to merge - well-structured refactoring with comprehensive test coverage - The changes are well-architected with proper error handling throughout. The new `lifecycle-hooks.ts` module cleanly separates concerns, all hook emission paths include error handling that logs warnings without failing execution, and the test coverage validates the core lifecycle hook behavior. The `toolCallId` propagation is handled consistently with proper fallbacks. - No files require special attention Last reviewed commit: d48824c

target.title: fix(doctor): show per-step progress spinners during update
target.body:
## Summary

- `openclaw doctor` offers a git update before running checks, but the update (fetch/rebase/build/ui:build/doctor) ran behind a single static note with zero progress indication for several minutes — it looked frozen, and users couldn't tell progress from a hang.
- This PR wires the existing `createUpdateProgress()` controller (already used by `openclaw update`) into the doctor pre-update call site, so each update step shows a live spinner and a completion line with duration.
- Spinners are disabled when stdout is not a TTY (`Boolean(process.stdout.isTTY)`), and `stop()` runs in a `finally` block so an updater error cannot leave an orphaned spinner.
- Intentionally out of scope: the npm/package-manager (non-git) update path and the update runner/progress controller themselves — call-site wiring only, no new progress implementation.
- Reviewers should focus on `src/commands/doctor-update.ts`; the runner already accepted optional `UpdateStepProgress`, so no contract changed.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes # — N/A (observed during normal usage; no tracking issue)

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related # — N/A

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No — contributor UX fix for a confusing silent wait.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: the `openclaw doctor` git update ran fetch/rebase/build/ui:build/doctor with no progress output after one static note, so a multi-minute update looked frozen.
- Real environment tested: macOS (Darwin 25.5.0, arm64), Node v22.22.1, real git source checkout of openclaw (built `dist`, run via `node openclaw.mjs`), starting at commit `fe4c48d` = this patch on top of `origin/main~4` (4 commits behind), pristine `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`; the update resolved its target to `418d7e1e` (the then-current `origin/main` tip).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: in that checkout, ran `node openclaw.mjs doctor` in a real terminal session, answered Yes to "Update OpenClaw from git before running doctor?", and let the full git update run through completion.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): redacted terminal capture from the live session (spinner completion lines with per-step durations):

```text
┌  OpenClaw doctor
◆  Update OpenClaw from git before running doctor?
│  ● Yes / ○ No
└
◇  Update OpenClaw from git before running doctor?
│  Yes
◇  Update ──────────╮
│                   │
│  Running update…  │
│                   │
├───────────────────╯
◇  ✓ Working directory is clean (188ms)
◇  ✓ Fetching latest changes (8.51s)
◇  ✓ Upstream branch exists (31ms)
◇  ✓ Resolving upstream commit (39ms)
◇  ✓ Enumerating candidate commits (36ms)
◇  ✓ Preparing preflight worktree (6.59s)
◇  ✓ preflight checkout (418d7e1e) (188ms)
◇  ✓ preflight deps install (418d7e1e) (34.22s)
◇  ✓ preflight build (418d7e1e) (542.6s)
◇  ✓ Cleaning preflight worktree (39.96s)
◇  ✓ Rebasing onto target commit (904ms)
◇  ✓ Installing dependencies (911ms)
◇  ✓ Building (662.23s)
◇  ✓ Building UI assets (4.16s)
◇  ✓ Running doctor checks (93.53s)
◇  ✓ Verifying update (31ms)
◇  Update result ─────────────────────╮
│                                     │
│  Status: ok                         │
│  Mode: git                          │
│  Root: /private/tmp/openclaw-proof  │
│                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────╯
└  Update completed (doctor already ran as part of the update).
```

- Observed result after fix: every update step rendered a live spinner while running and finished with a ✓ line and its duration; the previous silent multi-minute gap between the update note and the result note is gone.
- What was not tested: the npm/package-manager (non-git) update path — this change does not touch it.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: capture is copied live output from the pty session with ANSI control sequences stripped for readability; absolute durations vary with machine load.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): on current main the same flow prints only `Running update (fetch/rebase/build/ui:build/doctor)…` and then nothing until the final result note (see `src/commands/doctor-update.ts` on `main`).

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm test src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts` — 3 passed (commands project).
- `pnpm check` and `pnpm build` on the refreshed branch.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- New test in `src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts`: asserts `runGatewayUpdate` receives the progress callbacks and that the spinner `stop()` runs exactly once when the updater throws (the failure mode flagged in review).

What failed before this fix, if known?

- No automated failure — the gap was UX-only (silent multi-minute update). The new test guards the spinner-cleanup contract going forward.

If no test was added, why not?

- N/A (test added).

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes — doctor's git update now shows per-step spinners with durations instead of a single static note.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

What is the highest-risk area?

Spinner lifecycle when stdout is not a TTY or when the updater throws mid-run.

How is that risk mitigated?

Spinner creation is gated on `Boolean(process.stdout.isTTY)`; `stop()` is called in `finally`; a regression test covers the throw path; the happy path is shown in the real terminal capture above.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review / ClawSweeper re-review of the refreshed head (rebased onto current main, proof attached).

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Nothing on the author side.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Greptile: hardcoded `createUpdateProgress(true)` → now `Boolean(process.stdout.isTTY)` (resolved).
- Greptile + Codex P2: `stop()` not called if `runGatewayUpdate` throws → `try/finally` plus regression test (resolved).
- Codex P2 on `src/plugin-sdk/subpaths.test.ts`: outdated thread — this PR only touches `src/commands/doctor-update.ts`, and that test file was since replaced on `main` (resolved with explanation).

target.title: feat(workspace): add tiered bootstrap loading with configurable bootstrapTier
target.body:
Closes #22438

## Motivation

Bootstrap files consume LLM tokens on every session. For users with large workspaces, loading all files into every session — including sub-agents and cron jobs — wastes context window budget on files the agent never references. This PR introduces tiered loading so users can control how much context each session type receives, **reducing unnecessary token usage** while preserving persona consistency where needed.

## What

Introduces a three-tier bootstrap file loading system (`minimal` | `standard` | `full`) with a new `agents.defaults.bootstrapTier` configuration option. Both the `minimal` and `standard` tiers exclude hook-loaded extras (e.g. `packages/*/AGENTS.md` from the `bootstrap-extra-files` hook); `full` keeps everything.

## Why

The current binary split (main=all, subagent/cron=minimal) doesn't support use cases where subagents need persona context or where main sessions should be lightweight. This adds a clean, backward-compatible middle ground that lets users optimize their token budget per session type.

## How

- **`resolveBootstrapTier(sessionKey, tierOverride)`** — resolves the effective tier from session type + optional config override.
- **`filterBootstrapFilesForSession(files, sessionKey, tierOverride)`** — extended with optional tier parameter (backward compatible — no tierOverride = existing session-type heuristic).
- **Hook-source guard** — both `minimal` and `standard` filters check `file.source !== "hook"` before allowlist matching, so a hook-loaded extra whose basename happens to be in the allowlist cannot slip in. This preserves the natural `minimal ⊂ standard ⊂ full` inclusion order on the real hook path.
- **Config** — `agents.defaults.bootstrapTier` in the zod schema, TypeScript types, labels, and help text.

The `source: "root" | "hook"` provenance field on `WorkspaceBootstrapFile` is the discriminator: root-level bootstrap files keep flowing through `minimal` and `standard`; per-package extras registered via the `bootstrap-extra-files` hook are only reachable through `full`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: tiered bootstrap loading — `agents.defaults.bootstrapTier` (`minimal` | `standard` | `full`) controls which workspace files are injected into each session's context window. Closes #22438. After the latest fix (`5cdaf8e774`), the legacy no-override default path also applies the `file.source !== "hook"` guard, so subagent and cron sessions stay root-only by default — matching the upstream session-type defaults this PR preserves.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2 on Apple Silicon, Node 24.13.0, pnpm 11.2.2, against the fork branch tip at commit `5cdaf8e774`. A reproduction harness at the repo root (`repro-tiered-bootstrap.mts`, not committed) imports the real `src/agents/workspace.ts` from this branch and invokes `resolveBootstrapTier` and `filterBootstrapFilesForSession` against an explicit `WorkspaceBootstrapFile[]` containing both root-loaded files and hook-loaded extras whose basenames collide with the root allowlist.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `git checkout 5cdaf8e774` (this PR's head)
2. `pnpm install --prefer-offline --frozen-lockfile`
3. Author the reproduction harness at the repo root, importing `./src/agents/workspace.js` and calling the exported functions directly with a `WorkspaceBootstrapFile[]` shaped like a real workspace (root files + two hook-sourced `packages/*/AGENTS.md` entries).
4. `node ./node_modules/.bin/tsx repro-tiered-bootstrap.mts`

Evidence after fix:

Terminal capture, run live against commit `5cdaf8e774`:

Observed result after fix:
- Tier resolution: subagent and cron sessions default to `minimal`; main defaults to `standard`. Any session honors an explicit `tierOverride`.
- Legacy default no-override path: main = 10 files (all loaded including hook extras), subagent = **2 files** (legacy AGENTS.md + TOOLS.md root only — hook-sourced `packages/*/AGENTS.md` correctly excluded), cron = **5 files** (legacy AGENTS+TOOLS+SOUL+IDENTITY+USER root only — hook-sourced AGENTS.md correctly excluded). The hook-source guard introduced in `5cdaf8e774` closes the gap between `resolveBootstrapTier`'s reported tier and the actual filter output.
- Explicit `tier="minimal"`: 5 files (MINIMAL_BOOTSTRAP_ALLOWLIST root only). Hook-sourced files always excluded.
- Explicit `tier="standard"`: 8 files (STANDARD_BOOTSTRAP_ALLOWLIST root only). Hook-sourced files always excluded.
- Explicit `tier="full"`: 10 files (all loaded including hook extras).
- Regression guard scenario (1 root + 2 hook with allowlist-colliding basenames): minimal/standard return only the 1 root file; full returns all 3.
- Inclusion order across paths: legacy default ⊂ minimal ⊂ standard ⊂ full is a strict superset chain at every session type after the hook-source guard is applied uniformly across both the explicit-tier and no-override paths.

What was not tested: glob expansion of `loadExtraBootstrapFilesWithDiagnostics` against a live filesystem (the harness supplies the `WorkspaceBootstrapFile[]` directly to exercise the filter under study). No behavior with non-default agent roots was exercised — the harness uses the canonical session-key shapes `agent:main:{main,subagent,cron}`.


## Testing (supplemental)

`src/agents/workspace.tiered-bootstrap.test.ts` (14 it-blocks, all passing locally) covers the tier resolution and filtering behavior at the unit level, including the explicit `minimal ⊂ standard ⊂ full` inclusion guard added with the `2a3faf3151` fix. `src/hooks/bundled/bootstrap-extra-files/handler.test.ts` exercises the end-to-end hook path against real temp directories, including the updated assertion that hook-loaded `packages/persona/SOUL.md` does not slip into a subagent session.

## Changes

- `src/agents/workspace.ts` — `BootstrapTier` type, `MINIMAL_BOOTSTRAP_ALLOWLIST`, `STANDARD_BOOTSTRAP_ALLOWLIST`, `resolveBootstrapTier`, and `filterBootstrapFilesForSession`. Both `minimal` and `standard` filters apply the `file.source !== "hook"` guard.
- `src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts` — `source: "root"` provenance field on the root-load path.
- `src/agents/workspace.tiered-bootstrap.test.ts` — 14 it-blocks; new minimal-tier regression added alongside the existing standard-tier one.
- `src/hooks/bundled/bootstrap-extra-files/handler.ts` — re-applies the tier filter after extras are appended, honoring the configured `agents.defaults.bootstrapTier`.
- `src/hooks/bundled/bootstrap-extra-files/handler.test.ts` — refreshed subagent assertion to expect the corrected hook-exclusion behavior.
- `src/config/schema.help.ts` / `schema.labels.ts` / `types.agent-defaults.ts` / `zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts` — config plumbing for `agents.defaults.bootstrapTier`.
